open_security_issues.author: Doria77486
open_security_issues.body_snippet:
## Description
The `QlibDataset` class loads training/validation data from pickle files (`train_data.pkl` or `val_data.pkl`) using Python's `pickle.load`. This poses a remote code execution (RCE) risk if an attacker can supply a malicious `.pkl` file.
```python
class QlibDataset(Dataset):
    def __
open_security_issues.title: Security Issue: Unsafe deserialization in `QlibDataset`

entries.title: Unsafe pickle.load on training data — disclosed RCE unfixed for 95 days
entries.action_hint: Finetune users: do NOT run finetune/* with untrusted .pkl files. Apply PR #249 patch locally or wait for merge.
entries.threat_model_context: Issue #216 identifies finetune/dataset.py:42 calling pickle.load on train_data.pkl/val_data.pkl. Any attacker who controls the .pkl file — via shared dataset, collaboration repo, or supply of 'pretrained' data — achieves arbitrary code execution at load time. Fix PR #249 proposes a RestrictedUnpickler, sitting unreviewed.
entries.what_this_means: If you finetune Kronos on a `.pkl` file you did not generate locally, and that file was tampered with, running the dataset loader executes attacker-controlled Python. This is the canonical Python pickle-deserialization RCE class.
entries.what_this_does_not_mean: This does NOT affect the inference-only path. Users who only load models from Hugging Face (NeoQuasar/Kronos-*) and call KronosPredictor never invoke pickle.load on user data. The RCE surface is finetune/ exclusively.
entries.date_label: Disclosed 2026-01-15; fix unmerged as of scan
entries.how_to_fix: Consumer-side (finetune users): apply PR #249 locally as a patch, OR only run finetune/ code on .pkl files you generated yourself on the same machine. Maintainer-side: review + merge PR #249; publish a security advisory documenting the window.

entries.title: 48 known CVEs in runtime dependency graph, with loose/unpinned pinning
entries.action_hint: Pin deps in a project-local constraints file; re-scan dep graph with `pip-audit` at install time.
entries.threat_model_context: numpy (16 CVEs), torch (12), flask (8), flask-cors (8), tqdm (3), pandas (1) all have current osv.dev advisories. requirements.txt leaves numpy and pandas unpinned and torch at `>=2.0.0` — a fresh install may resolve to a vulnerable version or an older already-patched version depending on pip's resolver state. webui adds flask==2.3.3 (released 2023; superseded by fixed releases) and flask-cors==4.0.0 (multiple GHSAs against 4.x).
entries.what_this_means: On a fresh `pip install -r requirements.txt`, the dependency resolver may pull versions with published CVEs. Neither dependabot nor a lockfile gates this.
entries.what_this_does_not_mean: This is not evidence that Kronos itself exploits these CVEs. These are transitive advisory surfaces. Whether any individual CVE is reachable from Kronos's usage of numpy/torch is a separate, code-path question not investigated here.
entries.how_to_fix: Consumer-side: `pip-audit` after install; maintain a project-local constraints file that pins to known-clean versions. Maintainer-side: add dependabot config (.github/dependabot.yml), pin flask/flask-cors to patched versions, decide whether to pin or range-lock numpy/pandas/torch.

entries.title: Zero formal review + no branch protection + solo-maintainer signature
entries.action_hint: Treat master as 'maintainer's working branch' not 'reviewed code' — pin to a reviewed SHA before production use.
entries.threat_model_context: 0% formal-review rate and 5.3% any-review rate across the last 19 closed PRs. No classic branch protection, no rulesets, no CODEOWNERS. Maintainer shiyu-coder (Tsinghua PhD student) authors or merges the majority of commits directly. If the maintainer's credentials leak or the account is compromised, there is no second-human review gate before a malicious commit lands on master.
entries.what_this_means: Commits land on master without independent review. Research-repo norm, not a malicious signal, but structurally indistinguishable from a one-account supply-chain target.
entries.what_this_does_not_mean: This is not a claim of malicious behavior by the maintainer. It's a structural-risk observation about the review gate.
entries.how_to_fix: Maintainer-side: enable branch protection on master (classic or ruleset) requiring at least 1 review + status checks; add CODEOWNERS naming 2 reviewers. Consumer-side: pin installs to a specific SHA that you personally reviewed.

entries.title: Zero formal releases — installs resolve to master HEAD
entries.action_hint: Pin to a specific commit SHA for any reproducible install.
entries.threat_model_context: Kronos has never cut a release tag. `pip install -r requirements.txt` from a git clone resolves the installed code to whatever commit is on master at clone time. No semver, no changelog boundary, no rollback target. A regression or malicious commit reaches users immediately.
entries.what_this_means: There is no concept of 'Kronos v1.2.3' — every user installs whatever was on master when they cloned. Two users installing a week apart get different Kronos code.
entries.what_this_does_not_mean: This is not unusual for 10-month-old research repos. It IS a structural amplifier for F2 (review gap) because the lack of release boundary means unreviewed commits ship instantly.
entries.how_to_fix: Maintainer-side: cut even informal date-based tags (e.g., 2026.04) so downstreams have a rollback target. Consumer-side: `git clone && git checkout REVIEWED-SHA` instead of `git clone && pip install`.

entries.title: No SECURITY.md, no published advisories despite a known unfixed RCE
entries.action_hint: If you discover a vuln in Kronos, expect to report it via the public issue tracker only.
entries.threat_model_context: community/profile reports no SECURITY.md, no CONTRIBUTING, no code-of-conduct. Published-advisory count is 0. Issue #216 (95 days old, unfixed pickle RCE) was reported publicly with no silent-disclosure option. Any future vuln reporter will follow the same public-issue path — which, for a pre-patched RCE, is a leak by design.
entries.what_this_means: There is no private vulnerability-disclosure channel documented. Vulnerabilities reach the public before fixes do.
entries.what_this_does_not_mean: Not evidence that disclosure is impossible — GitHub's 'Report a vulnerability' button still exists under Security. But its absence from README/SECURITY.md means reporters may default to the public issue tracker.
entries.how_to_fix: Maintainer-side: add a 1-page SECURITY.md naming 'Report a vulnerability' button + expected response window; publish GHSA advisory retroactively for #216.

entries.title: No dependabot config, no CODEOWNERS, no formal CI beyond Copilot review
entries.threat_model_context: No .github/dependabot.yml. No CODEOWNERS. Single workflow is a Copilot PR reviewer (auto-review only). No test CI workflow gates merges. Combined with F2 this paints a picture of a research repo operated by a solo maintainer near capacity — not a production-supported project.
entries.what_this_means: Operational hygiene is minimal. Dep-graph updates do not arrive automatically; no enforced ownership; no automated test gate.
entries.what_this_does_not_mean: Not an exploitable vulnerability by itself. Signals the repo's investment level rather than a specific bug.
entries.how_to_fix: Maintainer-side: enable dependabot (security-alerts-only minimum); add one CODEOWNERS entry; add a trivial CI workflow that runs existing regression tests.

entries.claim: Open security issue #216 discloses unsafe pickle.load on QlibDataset training data — sitting 95 days unfixed; fix PR #249 sitting 7 days unreviewed.
entries.command: gh api repos/shiyu-coder/Kronos/issues/216; gh api repos/shiyu-coder/Kronos/pulls/249
entries.result: Issue #216 created 2026-01-15 (state=open, 1 comment, labels=[]). PR #249 'fix: replace unsafe pickle.load with restricted unpickler' created 2026-04-13 (state=open, not merged).

entries.claim: pickle.load exists at finetune/dataset.py:42 and pickle.dump calls at finetune/qlib_data_preprocess.py lines 115/117/119 — confirms the RCE vector described in #216.
entries.command: gh api repos/shiyu-coder/Kronos/contents/finetune/dataset.py | jq -r .content | base64 -d | grep -n pickle
entries.result:
finetune/dataset.py:1: import pickle
finetune/dataset.py:42:            self.data = pickle.load(f)
finetune/qlib_data_preprocess.py:2: import pickle
finetune/qlib_data_preprocess.py:115:            pickle.dump(train_data, f)

entries.claim: osv.dev enumerates 48 known CVEs across the runtime dependency graph: numpy (16), torch (12), flask (8), flask-cors (8), tqdm (3), pandas (1).
entries.result: numpy: 16 vulns; torch: 12; flask: 8; flask-cors: 8; tqdm: 3; pandas: 1. Total 48 advisories queryable via osv.dev for the unpinned/loose runtime dependency set.

entries.claim: requirements.txt entries are unpinned (numpy, pandas) or loosely pinned (torch >= 2.0.0) — mixed with exact pins (einops==0.8.1, safetensors==0.6.2). webui/requirements.txt similarly mixes pins.
entries.result: runtime dependencies (harness-parsed): numpy unpinned; pandas unpinned; torch >=2.0.0; einops ==0.8.1; huggingface_hub ==0.33.1; matplotlib ==3.9.3; safetensors ==0.6.2; flask ==2.3.3; flask-cors ==4.0.0; plotly ==5.17.0.

entries.claim: Zero formal releases — the repo has never cut a version tag; installers resolve to HEAD on master.
entries.result: total_count: 0; releases.entries: []. No git tags. Installation path is `git clone + pip install -r requirements.txt` against the current default-branch commit.

entries.claim: No branch protection at any layer — classic protection returns 404, rulesets count is 0, rules_on_default is 0.
entries.command: gh api repos/shiyu-coder/Kronos/branches/master/protection; gh api repos/shiyu-coder/Kronos/rulesets; gh api 'repos/shiyu-coder/Kronos/rules/branches/master'
entries.result: classic: HTTP 404; rulesets: {entries:[], count:0}; rules_on_default: {entries:[], count:0}. CODEOWNERS: not found at any standard location.

entries.claim: PR review rate is 0.0% formal and 5.3% any-review across the last 19 closed PRs — only 1 of 19 PRs received reviewer feedback before merge.
entries.result: sample_size: 19; formal_review_rate: 0.0; any_review_rate: 5.3 (1 of 19 PRs had 2 reviews — PR #224). self_merge_count: 0. Maintainer merges author-submitted PRs without independent review.

entries.claim: 25 open PRs include multiple unreviewed fixes from external contributors (including security-relevant ones) sitting 5-7 days stale.
entries.result: Open (age as of scan): #249 pickle.load fix (7d), #250 flexible dep ranges (7d), #244 'resolve 8 bugs in core model' (7d), #238 '3 silent bugs in sampling' (8d), #263 fix data leakage finetune (5d), #262 fix sampling top-k/top-p (5d), #266 save_pretrained safetensors (5d), #253 tensor-dims fix (6d), #248 encoder/decoder block count (7d). Plus #83 dataloader leakage fix (224 days).

entries.claim: Contributor concentration: shiyu-coder holds 36 of 61 top-contributor commits (59%); top-3 share is 52/61 (85%). Solo-maintainer author signature.
entries.result: shiyu-coder: 36; Luciferbobo: 9; CharlesJ-ABu: 7; AnMakc: 6; randyy179: 2; xxsunyxx: 1. Total contributor count: 19. Maintainer profile: PhD student at Tsinghua University per GitHub bio.

entries.claim: community/profile reports no SECURITY.md, no CONTRIBUTING, no CODE_OF_CONDUCT; published-advisory count is 0 despite the known pickle RCE (issue #216).
entries.result: health_percentage: 42; has_security_policy: false; has_contributing: false; has_code_of_conduct: false. security-advisories count: 0. Disclosure channel is neither documented nor exercised.

entries.claim: Model weights are hosted off-GitHub at Hugging Face under NeoQuasar/* — separate integrity and provenance surface from the repo.
entries.command: curl -sI https://huggingface.co/NeoQuasar/Kronos-base; gh api repos/shiyu-coder/Kronos/readme | jq -r .content | base64 -d | grep -i huggingface
entries.result: README references NeoQuasar/Kronos-mini, NeoQuasar/Kronos-small, NeoQuasar/Kronos-base, NeoQuasar/Kronos-Tokenizer-2k, NeoQuasar/Kronos-Tokenizer-base. Model-weight integrity depends on HuggingFace Hub access controls, not on this repo's governance.

entries.claim: Harness dangerous_primitives scan reported 0 hits in the deserialization family despite pickle.load being confirmed present at finetune/dataset.py:42 — coverage gap in the harness's regex family.
entries.result: deserialization: 0 hits across all scanned files. Authoritative evidence for the pickle RCE comes from issue #216 + direct gh api fetch of finetune/dataset.py, not from the harness primitive scan. Coverage-gap annotation surfaced in coverage_gaps.

section_01.pill_row: 19.7k⭐ · MIT · Python · 2 critical · 3 warning · 1 info · CRITICAL (split)
section_01.summary: Kronos is a 19.7k-star Python foundation model for financial candlestick forecasting, backed by AAAI-accepted research and Hugging Face weight distribution. The research artifact is legitimate; the operational posture is not. An unfixed 95-day-old pickle RCE on the finetuning path, a dependency graph with 48 CVEs, zero formal releases, no branch protection, and 0% formal PR review together make this Critical on the Deployment axis.

section_02.pill_row: F0 Critical · F1 Critical · F2 Warning · F3 Warning · F4 Warning · F5 Info

section_06.summary: All four scorecard cells land at red. Q1 and Q3 align with the Phase 3 advisory and need no override. Q2 and Q4 are ESCALATE overrides: Phase 3 compute returned amber on both because its signals are sparse (open_security_issue_count=1 alone is thin for Q2; q4_has_critical_on_default_path=False missed the pickle path entirely). Phase 4 sees the qualitative context — disclosed 95-day RCE, 48-CVE dep graph, no-release install model — and escalates Q2 to red (override_reason=signal_vocabulary_gap — the compute signal's domain doesn't include open issues) and Q4 to red (override_reason=harness_coverage_gap — the dangerous_primitives regex missed the pickle.load in finetune/dataset.py:42).

split_axis_decision.reasoning: The F0 pickle RCE lives on the finetune path (finetune/dataset.py). Inference-only users — who load pre-trained weights from Hugging Face (NeoQuasar/Kronos-*) and call KronosPredictor — do NOT traverse that code path. Their exposure is F1 (dep-graph CVEs) + F3 (master-HEAD install) + governance (F2/F4). Finetune users traverse the full risk surface including F0. Same repo, two consumer profiles with materially different risk.

entries.note: No pickle RCE exposure; still faces dep-graph CVEs + no-release install model.
Full risk surface including unfixed pickle RCE on dataset-loading path.

groups.headline: Critical — F0 + F1 are the blocking items for anyone finetuning Kronos
groups.one_line_summary: Unfixed RCE on finetune path + 48-CVE dependency graph.

items.claim: pickle.load on untrusted training data — disclosed 95 days, fix PR sitting 7 days.
48 CVEs in runtime deps; numpy/pandas unpinned, torch >=2.0.0.

groups.headline: Warning — F2 + F3 + F4 describe the governance posture
groups.one_line_summary: Solo-maintainer + no branch protection + no releases + no disclosure policy.

items.claim: 0% formal PR review, no branch protection, no CODEOWNERS.
Zero releases; installs resolve to master HEAD.
No SECURITY.md, 0 published advisories despite known RCE.

groups.headline: Info — F5 is hygiene, non-blocking
groups.one_line_summary: No dependabot, no CODEOWNERS, only a Copilot PR reviewer workflow.

items.claim: Minimal operational tooling — single-maintainer capacity signature.

selections.reason: Falsification of the 95-day open-RCE claim (e.g., #216 closed, #249 merged between scan and read) moves F0 from Critical to Warning/Info and lets Q2 fall back to amber without override.
Re-querying osv.dev after a requirements.txt pin-tightening (e.g., flask→2.3.x-latest, pandas pinned) may halve the advisory count and move F1 from Critical to Warning.
Enabling branch protection + CODEOWNERS + formal review would flip Q1 signals, collapse the governance floor, and move F2 severity.

entries.headline: Before installing: review issue #216 and PR #249
entries.non_technical: Kronos has a known, unfixed code-execution risk on the finetuning path. Read the issue and proposed fix before deciding whether this affects your workflow.
entries.command: gh api repos/shiyu-coder/Kronos/issues/216; gh api repos/shiyu-coder/Kronos/pulls/249

entries.headline: Pin to a specific reviewed commit SHA, not HEAD
entries.non_technical: Kronos has no releases. Cloning master means installing whatever landed today. Pick a commit you're comfortable with and pin to it.
entries.command: git clone https://github.com/shiyu-coder/Kronos && cd Kronos && git checkout 67b630e67f6a18c9e9be918d9b4337c960db1e9a

entries.headline: Install in a fresh virtualenv + audit dependency graph
entries.non_technical: The dependency graph has 48 known CVEs. Install into an isolated venv and audit with pip-audit before importing.
entries.command: python3 -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate && pip install -r requirements.txt && pip install pip-audit && pip-audit

entries.headline: If finetuning: apply PR #249 locally before running finetune/* code
entries.non_technical: The pickle.load RCE is on the finetuning path. If you plan to run any finetune/*.py scripts, patch finetune/dataset.py with the restricted-unpickler change from PR #249 BEFORE invoking it on any .pkl file you did not generate yourself.
entries.command: gh pr diff 249 --repo shiyu-coder/Kronos | git apply

entries.headline: Inference-only users: verify model downloads from HuggingFace integrity
entries.non_technical: If you are only running inference (loading a model from HuggingFace via KronosPredictor), the pickle RCE does not apply to you. Still — verify the HF model hashes match the NeoQuasar/* repo state before trusting them.

entries.label: Scan date
entries.note: #216 now 95 days open; #249 now 7 days open

executable_file_inventory.lead: Kronos ships Python source files executed via `pip install -r requirements.txt` + `python examples/*.py`. No curl-pipe installer, no shell-executable files classified by the harness (`executable_files: 0`).
executable_file_inventory.layer1_summary: Primary install vector is pip-install-requirements; primary code-execution surface is Python modules imported by the user.

layer2_entries.notes: QlibDataset loads train_data.pkl / val_data.pkl via pickle.load — disclosed RCE vector (issue #216).
Writes train_data.pkl / val_data.pkl consumed by dataset.py. Dump-side is not directly exploitable but creates the attack surface.

layer2_entries.network: Flask listens on a local port when run
layer2_entries.notes: Web UI surfaces forecasts via Flask. Dep-graph carries 16 CVEs (flask=8 + flask-cors=8).

executable_file_inventory.closing_note: No postinstall scripts, no curl-pipe installer, no shell-executable helpers. Risk surface is the Python code paths themselves, especially finetune/.

rows.result: ⚠ HTTP 403 (admin scope required; fell back to osv.dev)
⚠ 0 channels detected — harness does not model 'git clone + pip install' from master as a channel

⊗ Not available on this scanner host

entries: OSSF Scorecard not indexed — overall_score and per-check signals unavailable; governance/review signals derived from raw gh api data instead.
Dependabot alerts require admin scope (HTTP 403) — osv.dev used as substitute; dependabot-configured alerting is absent from this repo regardless.

Dangerous-primitives deserialization family reported 0 hits but pickle.load is confirmed present at finetune/dataset.py:42 (E12). Harness regex family missed it; authoritative evidence comes from issue #216 + direct file fetch. Pattern-family gap logged.
Distribution-channel harness does not classify 'git clone + pip install -r requirements.txt' as a channel — the 0/0 channel count is a coverage artifact, not evidence of 'no install path'.
Gitleaks secret-scanning not available on this scan host — secret-in-history check deferred.
Hugging Face model-weight integrity (NeoQuasar/*) is outside the scanner's scope — HF access controls + HF hub integrity determine actual artifact provenance.

does_anyone_check_the_code.short_answer: No — 0% formal review, no branch protection, no CODEOWNERS

do_they_fix_problems_quickly.short_answer: No — 95-day-old RCE issue #216 unfixed, fix PR #249 sitting 7 days unreviewed
do_they_fix_problems_quickly.rationale: The Phase 3 advisory computed amber because compute.py's Q2 signals are sparse: only `q2_open_security_issue_count=1` is populated, with `q2_oldest_open_cve_pr_age_days=None` and `q2_closed_fix_lag_days=None` (harness does not currently tag PRs as CVE-fixes or measure closed-PR lag against security-label filters). Phase 4 sees richer qualitative evidence: (1) issue #216 is an unambiguous RCE disclosed 2026-01-15 — 95 days unfixed as of scan; (2) the proposed fix PR #249 has been sitting open 7 days with zero reviewer engagement; (3) the broader PR queue shows 9 other security-relevant fixes from external contributors stuck in the same review starvation (E8); (4) the full dependency graph carries 48 osv.dev advisories with no dependabot gate. Red reflects 'the fix channel is not functioning', not just 'we found one issue'.
do_they_fix_problems_quickly.edge_case: Kronos is a research repo with a single maintainer; `fix-quickly` is plausibly outside the maintainer's operational capacity rather than a governance-intent failure. The red color reflects consumer-impact — fixes DON'T reach users quickly — not a judgment about maintainer effort.
do_they_fix_problems_quickly.suggested_threshold_adjustment: Add a q2_oldest_open_security_issue_age_days signal (would have caught the 95-day gap here without any override). Add a q2_open_security_fix_pr_age_days signal that measures the oldest open PR whose title matches security-fix keywords. Either would move this from 'amber via limited-data' to 'red via signal' without requiring Phase 4 override.

do_they_tell_you_about_problems.short_answer: No — no SECURITY.md, 0 advisories published despite open RCE issue #216

is_it_safe_out_of_the_box.short_answer: No — install from master + unpinned deps + disclosed RCE on finetune path
is_it_safe_out_of_the_box.rationale: The Phase 3 advisory computed amber because compute.py's Q4 signals report `q4_has_critical_on_default_path=False` and `q4_has_warning_on_install_path=False` (both derived from harness coverage that did not confirm a critical on the install path — the dangerous_primitives deserialization family missed the pickle.load at finetune/dataset.py:42; see E12 coverage gap). Phase 4 sees (1) an unambiguous disclosed RCE on the documented finetune path (F0); (2) the only install model is `pip install -r requirements.txt` from a master checkout with no release boundary (F3); (3) the dependency graph ships 48 known CVEs (F1) with loose/unpinned requirements; (4) no artifact verification of any kind — no SLSA, no signed wheels, no checksums. 'Out of the box safe' is not a defensible reading for a finetune user. Red reflects the realistic consumer experience on the documented default path.
is_it_safe_out_of_the_box.edge_case: Inference-only users (load from Hugging Face, call KronosPredictor) do not traverse the pickle RCE path — their exposure is the dependency-graph CVEs + master-HEAD install only. This is the basis for the Deployment-axis split (see Verdict).
is_it_safe_out_of_the_box.suggested_threshold_adjustment: Extend q4_has_critical_on_default_path to accept evidence from `security_advisories`+`issues_and_commits.open_security_issues` when title/body keyword-matches RCE/deserialization/injection/command-exec. The narrow code-pattern grep missed this one.

catalog_metadata.short_description: Python decoder-only foundation model for financial K-line data; Flask webui; model weights on Hugging Face; ships from master (no releases).
catalog_metadata.target_user: Quant researcher or finetuning user who wants to predict candlestick sequences. Install is `git clone + pip install -r requirements.txt`; models load from `NeoQuasar/Kronos-*` on HF.
catalog_metadata.shape: Python ML library + Flask webui + HF-hosted weights (new shape — outside the 5 prior V2.5-preview shapes zustand/caveman/Archon/markitdown/ghostty)
catalog_metadata.prior_scan_note: None — first scan of Kronos. Second wild V1.2-schema scan (after markitdown entry 15 and ghostty entry 16).

phase_4_structured_llm.timeline_intro: Key beats in Kronos's public lifecycle, anchored on the 95-day pickle-RCE window.
phase_4_structured_llm.evidence_intro: Command-backed evidence from the Phase 1 harness run + direct gh api queries + osv.dev lookups.
phase_4_structured_llm.coverage_intro: Phase 1 harness coverage with 6 documented gaps (OSSF, dependabot scope, dangerous-primitives deserialization miss, distribution-channel model, gitleaks, HF-weight provenance).

editorial_caption.text: Kronos is a legitimate AAAI-2026 research artifact for financial candlestick forecasting, 19.7k stars, MIT, Python. The science is real; the operational posture is not. A pickle RCE on the finetune data path (issue #216) has sat unfixed 95 days with its fix PR unreviewed 7 days. The dep graph carries 48 known CVEs. Zero releases — every install tracks master — with no branch protection and 0% formal PR review. Inference-only users (HF weights via KronosPredictor) avoid the pickle path; finetune users do not — the Deployment split reflects that gap.

body_paragraphs: Issue #216 was opened on 2026-01-15 by an external reporter who noticed that `QlibDataset` in `finetune/dataset.py` calls `pickle.load` on `train_data.pkl` and `val_data.pkl` files that the user is expected to generate with `finetune/qlib_data_preprocess.py`. Python's `pickle` module executes arbitrary code during deserialization — this is not a subtle vulnerability, it is the textbook RCE class that Python's own docs warn against.

A fix PR (#249) was opened on 2026-04-13 proposing a `RestrictedUnpickler` that whitelists safe classes. As of the scan, PR #249 has been open for 7 days with zero reviewer engagement — unremarkable for this repo's PR queue, where 9 other security-relevant fixes from external contributors have sat 5-7 days in the same state (E8). The issue itself has been open 95 days.

For consumers: if you plan to run anything under `finetune/`, you need to treat every `.pkl` file as potentially executable. The safe paths are (a) only load `.pkl` files you generated on the same machine in this session, (b) apply PR #249 locally before running any finetune code, or (c) stick to the inference-only usage mode (loading models from Hugging Face via `KronosPredictor`), which does not traverse this code path.
This finding is what drives the Critical verdict on the finetune-deployment split. Without it, the verdict collapses to Warning on governance + dependency hygiene.

`pip install -r requirements.txt` resolves a dependency graph that currently carries 48 published advisories on osv.dev: numpy (16), torch (12), flask (8), flask-cors (8), tqdm (3), pandas (1). The quality of that install depends on the resolver's state and when you ran it.

Two classes of pinning problem compound the CVE load. Numpy and pandas are fully unpinned in the top-level requirements.txt, leaving the resolver free to pick either a known-vulnerable older release or a post-patch release depending on environment state. Torch is pinned only at `>=2.0.0`, which spans multiple major versions with different CVE exposures. Even the exactly-pinned entries (flask==2.3.3, flask-cors==4.0.0) resolve to versions with published CVEs in their own right.

Neither a dependabot config nor a project-level lockfile gates this. The maintainer has no automated way to know which advisories apply to the current graph. Consumer mitigation is straightforward: run `pip-audit` against your `pip freeze` output in a fresh virtualenv; if it surfaces anything critical in your actual execution path, override-pin those packages in a project-local constraints file before trusting the install.

Across the last 19 closed-and-merged PRs the formal-review rate is 0% and the any-review rate is 5.3% — PR #224 (a webui numpy-pin cleanup) is the only PR in the sample that drew even an informal comment. Every other PR was merged by the maintainer without independent review.

The repo has no classic branch protection (`gh api repos/shiyu-coder/Kronos/branches/master/protection` returns 404), no rulesets (count=0), no CODEOWNERS, and no test-gating CI workflow — the single workflow is a Copilot PR reviewer that produces advisory output, not a merge gate. Combined with a 59% commit share for the maintainer, this is a structurally single-point-of-trust repository: a leaked credential or a compromised maintainer account lands a malicious commit on master with no second-human or automated check.

None of this is evidence of current malice. It IS evidence that the repository's operational model does not include independent review, and consumers who depend on Kronos in production contexts should plan around that — minimally by pinning to a reviewed SHA rather than tracking master.

Kronos has never cut a release tag. `gh api repos/shiyu-coder/Kronos/releases` returns `total_count: 0`. The documented install path in the README is `pip install -r requirements.txt` against a fresh clone — meaning the installed code is literally whatever commit was on master at clone time.

The absence of releases is an amplifier for F2. In projects with versioned releases, the governance gap at HEAD matters less to consumers because they can pin to a reviewed tag. Here, HEAD is the only reachable version. Regressions and malicious commits both ship instantly to anyone who clones after they land.

The fix on the consumer side is trivial (`git checkout REVIEWED-SHA` before the pip install). The fix on the maintainer side — cutting even informal date-based tags (`2026.04`) so downstreams have a rollback target — is also trivial but has not happened in 10 months.

`community/profile` reports no SECURITY.md, no CONTRIBUTING, no CODE_OF_CONDUCT; health percentage 42. The security-advisories endpoint reports 0 published advisories — despite the known pickle RCE in issue #216, which arrived via the public issue tracker and has remained publicly visible and unfixed for 95 days.

The disclosure posture here is effectively 'everything is public'. A reporter with a pre-patched RCE has nowhere to go except the issue tracker, which means any future vulnerability is public before a fix exists. This is a process gap rather than a code defect — but it turns every finding into a race condition between disclosure and exploitation.

The fix is a 1-page SECURITY.md naming the 'Report a vulnerability' button under GitHub's Security tab, plus a retroactive GHSA advisory for #216 documenting the patch once #249 merges. Neither has happened.

No `.github/dependabot.yml`, no CODEOWNERS, no test-gating CI workflow — the only workflow is a Copilot PR reviewer. This is operational-capacity signal rather than a specific vulnerability. Combined with F2-F4 it paints a consistent picture of a solo-maintainer research repository operating at the edge of its capacity, not a production-supported project. Non-blocking, but useful context for organizations evaluating adoption.
